narrative: OMX30 faces a challenging open on Wednesday as global equity sentiment turns sour: DAX -0.27%, CAC -0.47%, and especially NASDAQ -0.90% signal broad risk-off in tech and growth. Technical deterioration is pronounced—MACD has crossed below signal line (hist=-3.229), RSI at 43.41 is neutral but vulnerable, and Stochastic K at 15.2 indicates deeply oversold conditions that could trigger sharp mean-reversion selling. The index remains +7.7% above 200-MA but has slipped -0.3% below 50-MA, straddling support. USD/SEK +0.75% supports exporters (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Ericsson), but weak ADX (6.3) undermines trend confidence. US 10Y yield +41bp aids banks, yet oil's -5.41% collapse and overnight NASDAQ weakness will weigh on sentiment. Watch for capitulation at ~3085 support.
morningBrief: Open cautiously NEUTRAL-to-BEARISH. Overnight selloff (NASDAQ -90bp, DAX -27bp) and MACD bearish crossover signal short-term downside into support at 3085 (lower Bollinger band). Stochastic K at 15.2 is extreme—expect potential flash recovery/squeeze, but ADX weakness (6.3) means any bounce lacks conviction. Industrials (Volvo, Atlas Copco) benefit from USD/SEK +75bp, but Tech/Telecom (Ericsson, Hexagon) face NASDAQ sentiment drag. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) catch a bid on US 10Y +41bp and wider NIM outlook. Pharma (AstraZeneca, Essity) should trade defensively on risk-off. Oil down -5.4% removes some input cost pressure—monitor for consumer stabilization. Key watch: can we hold 3085? If broken, target 3050. US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) could reignite volatility if hot. Reduce leverage, take risk off table; structure for mean-reversion scalps.
drivers: Broad selloff across DAX, CAC, NASDAQ (-0.90%) signals risk-off momentum
MACD bearish crossover with negative histogram (-3.229) confirms downside pressure
Stochastic deeply oversold (K=15.2) flags mean reversion risk to downside before recovery
USD/SEK strength (+0.75%) provides modest exporter tailwind but insufficient offset
Oil heavy decline (-5.41%) reduces input cost inflation; mixed for industrials vs consumer

sectorImpacts.reason: USD/SEK +0.75% provides FX tailwind for exports, but global equity weakness and oil -5.41% create offsetting drag. Net modest positive from FX.
US 10Y yield +41bp to 4.354% expands NIM expectations. Defensible on broad risk-off as credit spread support.
NASDAQ -0.90% overnight and weak sentiment flow pressures valuation. DCF compression from higher rates also headwind.
Defensive rotation into safety on risk-off; yield pressure is secondary benefit for stable cash flows.
Gold +0.18% and copper +1.71% support Boliden directly. Oil down -5.41% helps SSAB on input costs.
EUR/SEK +0.55% increases sourcing costs; risk-off sentiment pressures discretionary. Oil decline offers minimal offset.
US 10Y +41bp translates to higher Swedish mortgage yields; refinancing pressure outweighs any beta-off benefit.

watchList.reason: Copper +1.71% and gold +0.18% provide direct revenue support. Oversold stochastic creates recovery opportunity if OMX stabilizes.
US 10Y +41bp widens Swedish bank NIM. Relative safety play on risk-off; support building into rate guidance.
USD/SEK +0.75% aids export pricing; avoid until OMX stabilizes above 3100 support.
NASDAQ -0.90% overnight and tech sentiment weakness. MACD bearish crossover adds headwind. Wait for reversal confirmation.
Defensive safe-haven trade on risk-off. Monitor for accumulation if OMX breaks lower. Yield pressure is manageable for pharma.

keyLevels.support: 3085 (lower Bollinger band, critical); 3050 (technical breakdown if broken)
keyLevels.pivotWatch: 3123 (Tuesday close); hold above = stabilization, break = acceleration risk

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) is primary catalyst. Swedish releases minimal today. Monitor for any ECB speak on policy persistence.
topRisk: Further deterioration in global risk sentiment (NASDAQ re-test of lows, geopolitical escalation on OPEC/UAE tensions, or surprise hawkish Fed signals) could breach 3085 support and trigger OMX -1.5% to -2.0% correction. Stochastic oversold creates whipsaw risk; stop-losses essential.
technicalSummary: Below 50-MA (-0.3%); still above 200-MA (+7.7%) — constructive but fragile
MACD bearish crossover with negative histogram -3.229 — downside momentum confirmed
RSI 43.41 neutral, Stochastic K 15.2 deeply oversold — mean-reversion risk to downside, then recovery
ADX 6.3 weak (reduce confidence 30%) — breakouts lack conviction; whipsaws likely
Bollinger %B 0.44 in lower half — support holds but volatility elevated (width 10.38%)
ROC-90 +8.0% still strong — intermediate momentum intact, but short-term negation via MACD
